    Purchase options and add-ons

    In the novel, "Siddhartha," a young man, leaves his family for a contemplative life, then, restless, discards it for one of the flesh. He conceives a son, but bored and sickened by lust and greed, moves on again. Near despair, Siddhartha comes to a river where he hears a unique sound. This sound signals the true beginning of his life -- the beginning of suffering, rejection, peace, and, finally, wisdom.

      Editorial Reviews

      From the Back Cover

      In the novel, "Siddhartha, a young man, leaves his family for a contemplative life, then, restless, discards it for one of the flesh. He conceives a son, but bored and sickened by lust and greed, moves on again. Near despair, Siddhartha comes to a river where he hears a unique sound. This sound signals the true beginning of his life -- the beginning of suffering, rejection, peace, and, finally, wisdom.

      About the author

      Follow authors for new release and deal updates, plus improved recommendations. See updates from all followed authors in Your Books.

      Hermann Hesse

      Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.
      Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.

      Hermann Hesse (1877-1962) was born in Germany and later became a citizen of Switzerland. As a Western man profoundly affected by the mysticism of Eastern thought, he wrote many novels, stories, and essays that bear a vital spiritual force that has captured the imagination and loyalty of many generations of readers. In 1946, he won the Nobel Prize for Literature for The Glass Bead Game.

      Photo by unknown [Dutch National Archives, The Hague, Fotocollectie Algemeen Nederlands Persbureau (ANEFO), 1945-1989 / Public Domain] [CC BY-SA 3.0 nl (http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/nl/deed.en)], via Wikimedia Commons.

      How customer reviews and ratings work

      Customer Reviews, including Product Star Ratings help customers to learn more about the product and decide whether it is the right product for them.

      To calculate the overall star rating and percentage breakdown by star, we don’t use a simple average. Instead, our system considers things like how recent a review is and if the reviewer bought the item on Amazon. It also analyzed reviews to verify trustworthiness.

      Learn more how customers reviews work on Amazon
      Sponsored

      Customers say

      Customers find that "Siddhartha" is beautifully written with poetic prose, offering deep spiritual lessons and themes of spiritual growth. The book tells a tale of enlightenment, and, for example, one customer notes it's a beautiful retelling of the Buddha's story. Customers describe the book as poignant and heartwarming, and appreciate its timeless nature. Opinions are split on whether the book is enjoyable to read, with some customers finding it engaging while others say they were bored throughout.

      Customers find that "Siddhartha" is enjoyable and easy to read, with several mentioning they can go through it multiple times. For example, one customer describes it as a must-read classic, while another notes it's required reading for all humans.
      "...The book is an easy read much can be gained--especially if the reader interprets the text from a western perspective." Read more

      "Eternal philosophy for the one looking to reflect. A classic and must read for those looking to think about their inward disposition." Read more

      Customers praise the spiritual insights in "Siddhartha", describing it as a thought-provoking guide to enlightenment and spiritual growth that leaves a lasting impression. For example, one customer notes its profound exploration of the human experience, while another mentions it serves as a good reminder of life's purpose.
      "Journey of inner piece, beautiful read which is thought provoking. If you are seeking some meaning in life, what a beautiful way to start :)" Read more
      "...Overall, "Siddhartha" is a profound and deeply moving novel that is sure to resonate with readers of all ages and backgrounds...." Read more
      "...Tale of enlightenment. It's about learning to trust and believe in yourself. Highly recommended." Read more
      "...what Budda went through to reach awakening, gave me an amazing understanding of the path. The path toward awakening...." Read more
      30 customers mention story quality, 27 positive, 3 negative30 customers mention "Story quality"27 positive3 negative
      Customers appreciate the story in "Siddhartha", describing it as an engaging tale of enlightenment. For example, one customer notes it's a beautiful retelling of the Buddha's story, while another highlights how the protagonist's journey unfolds as a personal unfolding process.
      "The story was nice, but I found the phraseology a bit repetitive, a bit boring, a bit meditative, with all those endless repetitions but I finally..." Read more
      "...But the story is amazing and it had been on my list for the longest time!..." Read more
      "Nice story that juxtaposes the Buddha. It is like a sideline to the Buddha's teachings and was pleasant easy reading...." Read more
      "Great story!" Read more
      14 customers mention writing style, 14 positive, 0 negative14 customers mention "Writing style"14 positive0 negative
      Customers appreciate the writing style of "Siddhartha", praising its beautiful and poetic prose. For example, one customer notes that the author writes with compassion, while another mentions that several books were written more than a century ago.
      "...Hesse was a talented writer who explored some of the pulsing questions of life...." Read more
      "...Hesse's writing style is simple and direct, and this is an easy read once the reader gets into the author's rhythm...." Read more
      "...It must be read and experienced. The writer's poetic prose is in keeping with the subject matter. As a novice Buddhist, I get it...." Read more
      "...are softer, and some more rigid, but either way, the story, and writing is masterful and captivating...." Read more

      "It was boring and extremely repetitive, forced to read it for english class because my teacher is a die hard buhdist follower...." Read more
      "This book is enjoyable. A friend recommended that I read it and I am so glad that I did. This translation is really great, almost no errors at all!" Read more
      "The story was nice, but I found the phraseology a bit repetitive, a bit boring, a bit meditative, with all those endless repetitions but I finally..." Read more

      Good for Beginners
      Great book for beginners curious about Buddhism. I do, however, remember reading this for the first time in high school and was very confused, so don’t be discouraged!!!

      Top reviews from the United States

      There was a problem filtering reviews. Please reload the page.

      • Joachim Buarø
        5.0 out of 5 stars "Siddhartha" is a masterpiece
        Reviewed in the United States on April 16, 2023
        Format: KindleVerified Purchase
        "Siddhartha" tells the story of a young man's journey toward enlightenment. This novel is a profound exploration of the human experience, as it explores themes such as spiritual growth, self-discovery, and the pursuit of happiness. Hesse's writing style is both elegant and poetic.

        "Siddhartha" has the power to transform readers and change their lives for the better. The story follows Siddhartha, a young man who sets out on a journey to find enlightenment, but finds that the path to true wisdom is fraught with obstacles and challenges. Along the way, he meets a range of characters who help him to learn and grow, and ultimately he discovers the true meaning of life.

        One of the most striking aspects of "Siddhartha" is its focus on the importance of personal growth and self-discovery. Hesse reminds us that we all have the potential to achieve greatness, but that we must be willing to confront our fears and weaknesses in order to do so. The novel encourages us to embrace our unique talents and gifts and to live our lives with purpose and intention.

        Overall, "Siddhartha" is a profound and deeply moving novel that is sure to resonate with readers of all ages and backgrounds. Hesse's writing is both beautiful and insightful, and his exploration of the human experience is both timeless and universal. Whether you are seeking spiritual enlightenment, or simply looking for a great work of literature to read, "Siddhartha" is an absolute must-read.
        Read more

      • Kent R. Spillner
        5.0 out of 5 stars Deep and Moving
        Reviewed in the United States on November 2, 2025
        Verified Purchase
        A very deep, very moving book. The peace, calm, and serenity Siddhartha finds at the end is extraordinarily beautiful. A truly wonderful book! I cherished reading it and look forward to reading it again someday.

      • Words, Images, & Worlds
        4.0 out of 5 stars Hesse's Masterwork
        Reviewed in the United States on July 27, 2017
        Format: KindleVerified Purchase
        A beautiful, haunting novel of spiritual growth and development. Hesse was a talented writer who explored some of the pulsing questions of life. His book on his experience in school, Beneath the Wheel, is one of my favorites. Siddhartha is probably the work to start with when reading Hesse (it's one of his most well-known), if not Demian or Steppenwolf before working up to The Glassbead Game.

        What makes this story so engaging (without sharing too much) is the way the ending reflects the beginning. This narrative arch marks this book as a masterwork and calls the reader to question long after reading.
        Read more

      • Amazon Customer
        5.0 out of 5 stars An essential read
        Reviewed in the United States on March 26, 2024
        Verified Purchase
        There are several translations from the original German on the market, and each one will appeal to a different audience. Reviews suggest that some are softer, and some more rigid, but either way, the story, and writing is masterful and captivating. I read a 1951 translation, I bought this one for my sister, who says it is exceeding expectations, and my wife and son are reading a Danish language translation. Many people at church have read it, and rave about it, and I can't praise the accessible wisdom and enjoyable reading enough. Just as most people say, it is life changing. Read it one way or another.
        Read more

      • Yan Tougas
        3.0 out of 5 stars Great book, mediocre translation
        Reviewed in the United States on August 30, 2015
        Verified Purchase
        Beautiful, clever story of Siddhartha, who follows a path similar to the Buddha's, and finds peace. The quality of the translation didn't match that of the story and I found it distracting. When I read this book again, I will most likely seek another version.

      • Morgan
        5.0 out of 5 stars Great book, deceiving title
        Reviewed in the United States on September 1, 2012
        Format: KindleVerified Purchase
        This is a great little book, especially if you happen to be going through a spiritual or midlife crisis such as I am right now. It's written with a compassion and an understanding of the human heart that is rarely seen in contemporary literature. What baffles me is why the author would intentionally title the book to mislead people into thinking it was the story of Siddartha Gautama, the actual Buddha. This book chronicles the life story of another man, a contemporary of the Buddha, who was also named Siddhartha. That just seems rather silly to me, and I see no reason why the author couldn't have given the protagonist a different name (unless he wanted to convey the message that we ALL possess the Buddha nature). I think the book should at least have a preface which explains that the following story is not the life story of the historical Buddha. I believe I understand why the author didn't focus on the life story of Gautama himself: because that is not what is important. Most of the details of his life are probably more myth than fact, so the author leaves the myth aside and focuses instead on the mundane experiences of one simple man in a search for wisdom. I won't spoil anything for the potential reader except to say that by the book's end he or she will have plenty of food for thought.
        Read more
        One person found this helpful

      • Frankie Muñiz
        5.0 out of 5 stars Phenomenal Quick Read; What is your path?
        Reviewed in the United States on April 27, 2023
        Verified Purchase
        I came late into the game when in comes to Hesse, but I feel as though it is never too late to take a shot at this book. The story is simple, but deeply meaningful. What is your path, how do you go about, what are you willing to bear and sacrifice. What is enlightenment, and how do we achieve this? Closure. All themes this magnificent short read will dip into and leave with a certain sense of understanding. Run with that. Cannot recommend this book enough to those who love to have some philosophical perspective in their reads.
        Read more

      • 購入者
        5.0 out of 5 stars 生涯に残る作品です
        Reviewed in Japan on July 13, 2004
        Verified Purchase
        １０代の時に一度ひょんな機会で巡り会い、衝撃を受けた作品。思春期は多感な時期で、自分は何者だろう、自分の人生はどういう意味があるんだろう、どうやって生きれば自分らしい人生をおくることができるだろう、と悩んでいた時に、この作品がガツン、と殴り込みをかけるかのように自分の生き方、考え方に激しく影響したのでした。
        その頃から「自分の人生に一番影響を及ぼした作品」としてこの一冊を挙げてましたが、この度、再び同じ版を購入することができ、再度読んでみたところ、再び開眼した、というほど大袈裟ではなくても、その時に気づかなかったことに気づくことができ、また生きる目的、幸せとは何か、を考えることになりました。西欧の作品なのに東洋の文化が取り上げられてるということで珍しいとされてるそうですが、それだけではなく、「自己淘汰」という普遍的なテーマをうまく扱ってる作品です。
        Read more

      • Chris
        5.0 out of 5 stars Simple, beautiful, and surprisingly powerful.
        Reviewed in the United Kingdom on November 26, 2025
        Verified Purchase
        What I loved:
        A beautifully written, calming book that somehow manages to feel simple and deep at the same time. It’s one of those rare stories that slows your brain down in a good way. Following Siddhartha’s journey feels like taking a quiet walk with your own thoughts — but with better scenery.

        What made me sigh loudly:
        Realising I am not nearly as wise or peaceful as I thought I was before reading this. Thanks, Hesse.

        Should you buy it?
        Absolutely. It’s short, meaningful, and surprisingly easy to read. If you like books that make you think about life, purpose, happiness, or why your mind never shuts up, this is a great pick.

        Beautiful writing.
        Calming message.
        A reflective journey without being pretentious.

        Would 100% recommend — even if you’re not normally into philosophy.
        Read more
        Report
